DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ORLANDO'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS MADE THE REQUIRED SHOWING FOR PRODUCTION OF THE WU PERSONNEL FILE UNDER BRITT V. SUPERIOR COURT (1978) 20 C3D. 844, 859-862. DESPITE THE FACT THAT THE REQESTED WITNESS STATEMENTS TAKEN BY DEFENDANTS' EMPLOYEES WERE LATER TRANSMITTED TO DEFENDANTS' ATTORNEYS, THEY DO NOT FALL UNDER THE ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE NACHT & LEWIS ARCHITECTS INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT, (1996) 47 CA 4TH 214, 218. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; NOT REPORTED

DECLARATION OF CHRISTOPHER J BEEMAN, ESQ IN SUPPORT OF DEFT'S REPLY TO PLTF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT SUPPERCLUB SAN FRANCISCO, LLC

DEFENDANT SUPPERCLUB SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT HAD NO ADVANCED KNOWLEDGE OF WU'S UNFITNESS. MOREOVER DEFENDANT REFUSED TO APPROVE OR RATIFY WU'S CONDUCT BY IMMEDIATELY TERMINATING HIM. FINALLY, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY PLAINTIFF THAT SHOW'S THAT DEFENDANT WAS PERSONALLY GUILTY OF OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET HIS BURDEN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797 (302)

Appearances: Christofer C. Nolan of Brent Fiol & Nolan LLP for plaintiff and moving party; Jenny Hughes Fitzgerald of Clapp Moroney Bellagamba Vucinich Beeman Scheley (925.734.0990) for defendant and responding party.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: defendant to verify information as it was known at that time, and not as it is known today. The court set a hearing on September 21, 2011 for defendant's request to amend responses. Defendant to file a supplemental brief, to which plaintiff's opposition will be filed by September 14, 2011, and defendant's reply will be filed by September 19, 2011. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-31-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SUPPERCLUB SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM FOR RELIEF IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT DEFENDANT HAD NO ADVANCED KNOWLEDGE OF WU'S UNFITNESS. MOREOVER DEFENDANT REFUSED TO APPROVE OR RATIFY WU'S CONDUCT BY IMMEDIATELY TERMINATING HIM. FINALLY, THERE IS NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED BY PLAINTIFF THAT SHOW'S THAT DEFENDANT WAS PERSONALLY GUILTY OF OPPRESSION, FRAUD OR MALICE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET HIS BURDEN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel The Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF MATTHEW ORLANDO'S MOTION TO COMPEL THE PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS.

APPEARANCES: Christofer C. Nolan, Esq. of Brent & Fiol, LLP 984-0416 for pltf & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - granted. Plaintiff has made the required showing for production of the Wu personnel file under Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 C3d. 844, 859-862. Despite the fact that the reqested witness statements taken by defendants' employees were later transmitted to defendants' attorneys, they do not fall under the attorney-client privilege Nacht & Lewis Architects Inc. v. Superior Court, (1996) 47 CA 4th 214, 218. Order signed in open court. Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Not reported
